title: Optimal Fees for Liquidity Provision in Automated Market Makers
borrowed_mechanism: Threshold-like or regime-shaped fee schedules instead of broad always-on carry.

title: FLAIR: A Metric for Liquidity Provider Competitiveness in Automated Market Makers
borrowed_mechanism: Separate competitiveness from adverse-selection loss instead of treating one fee level as good for both.

title: AMMs and Market Makers: DeFi and TradFi Through the Lens of Market Microstructure
borrowed_mechanism: Translate market-making controls into AMM fee rules instead of reasoning only from AMM canon.

title: High-frequency trading in a limit order book
borrowed_mechanism: Quote asymmetry should respond to adverse-selection risk and inventory state rather than broad symmetric spread moves.

title: High-frequency market-making with inventory constraints and directional bets
borrowed_mechanism: Use non-symmetric quoting when directional evidence is strong, but keep a bounded risk budget.

title: Flow Toxicity and Liquidity in a High Frequency World
borrowed_mechanism: Track toxicity from order-flow imbalance persistence and use it to throttle liquidity provision before losses widen.

title: Algorithmic Pricing and Liquidity in Securities Markets
borrowed_mechanism: Pricing rules can become too coordinated or too inert; explicit control on adaptation matters for competitiveness.

title: RFC 2698: A Two Rate Three Color Marker
borrowed_mechanism: Use bounded token-bucket style rate control to meter scarce rebate budget instead of leaving discounts continuously available.

title: Switched Optimal Control and Dwell Time Constraints: A Preliminary Study
borrowed_mechanism: Impose dwell-time constraints on switching so competitive and protective modes do not chatter.

title: SAC-MoE: Reinforcement Learning with Mixture-of-Experts for Control of Hybrid Dynamical Systems with Uncertainty
borrowed_mechanism: Use an interpretable router over simple experts when latent modes switch and one monolithic controller saturates.
